Content:
# Diffusion-SDF: Text-to-Shape via Voxelized Diffusion

Created by [Muheng Li](https://ttlmh.github.io/), [Yueqi Duan](https://duanyueqi.github.io/), [Jie Zhou](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=6a79aPwAAAAJ&hl=en&authuser=1), and [Jiwen Lu](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=TN8uDQoAAAAJ&hl=en&authuser=1).

![intro](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ttlmh/Diffusion-SDF/master/media/intro.png)
Translation:
# Diffusion-SDF: De texto a forma mediante difusión voxelizada

Creado por [Muheng Li](https://ttlmh.github.io/), [Yueqi Duan](https://duanyueqi.github.io/), [Jie Zhou](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=6a79aPwAAAAJ&hl=en&authuser=1), y [Jiwen Lu](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=TN8uDQoAAAAJ&hl=en&authuser=1).

![intro](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ttlmh/Diffusion-SDF/master/media/intro.png)

RowTranslations.translatedContent: # Diffusion-SDF: De texto a forma mediante difusión voxelizada

Content: We propose a new generative 3D modeling framework called **Diffusion-SDF** for the challenging task of text-to-shape synthesis. Previous approaches lack flexibility in both 3D data representation and shape generation, thereby failing to generate highly diversified 3D shapes conforming to the given text descriptions. To address this, we propose a SDF autoencoder together with the Voxelized Diffusion model to learn and generate representations for voxelized signed distance fields (SDFs) of 3D shapes. Specifically, we design a novel UinU-Net architecture that implants a local-focused inner network inside the standard U-Net architecture, which enables better reconstruction of patch-independent SDF representations. We extend our approach to further text-to-shape tasks including text-conditioned shape completion and manipulation. Experimental results show that Diffusion-SDF is capable of generating both high-quality and highly diversified 3D shapes that conform well to the given text descriptions. Diffusion-SDF has demonstrated its superiority compared to previous state-of-the-art text-to-shape approaches.
Translation: We propose a new generative 3D modeling framework called **Diffusion-SDF** for the challenging task of text-to-shape synthesis. Previous approaches lack flexibility in both 3D data representation and shape generation, thereby failing to generate highly diversified 3D shapes conforming to the given text descriptions. To address this, we propose a SDF autoencoder together with the Voxelized Diffusion model to learn and generate representations for voxelized signed distance fields (SDFs) of 3D shapes. Specifically, we design a novel UinU-Net architecture that implants a local-focused inner network inside the standard U-Net architecture, which enables better reconstruction of patch-independent SDF representations. We extend our approach to further text-to-shape tasks including text-conditioned shape completion and manipulation. Experimental results show that Diffusion-SDF is capable of generating both high-quality and highly diversified 3D shapes that conform well to the given text descriptions. Diffusion-SDF has demonstrated its superiority compared to previous state-of-the-art text-to-shape approaches.

Specifically, we design a novel UinU-Net architecture that implants a local-focused inner network inside the standard U-Net architecture, which enables better reconstruction of patch-independent SDF representations. We extend our approach to further text-to-shape tasks including text-conditioned shape completion and manipulation. Experimental results show that Diffusion-SDF is capable of generating both high-quality and highly diversified 3D shapes that conform well to the given text descriptions. RowTranslations.translatedContent: We propose a new generative 3D modeling framework called **Diffusion-SDF** for the challenging task of text-to-shape synthesis. Previous approaches lack flexibility in both 3D data representation and shape generation, thereby failing to generate highly diversified 3D shapes conforming to the given text descriptions. To address this, we propose a SDF autoencoder together with the Voxelized Diffusion model to learn and generate representations for voxelized signed distance fields (SDFs) of 3D shapes. Specifically, we design a novel UinU-Net architecture that implants a local-focused inner network inside the standard U-Net architecture, which enables better reconstruction of patch-independent SDF representations. We extend our approach to further text-to-shape tasks including text-conditioned shape completion and manipulation. Experimental results show that Diffusion-SDF is capable of generating both high-quality and highly diversified 3D shapes that conform well to the given text descriptions. Diffusion-SDF has demonstrated its superiority compared to previous state-of-the-art text-to-shape approaches.

## Code

### Installation
To set up the Diffusion-SDF environment, you can use the provided `diffusionsdf.yml` file to create a Conda environment. Follow the steps below:
1. Clone the repository:

## Código

### Instalación
Para configurar el entorno Diffusion-SDF, puede usar el archivo `diffusionsdf.yml` proporcionado para crear un entorno Conda. Siga los siguientes pasos:
1. Clone el repositorio:

```

```

```

```

Content: 2. Create the Conda environment using the provided YAML file and activate:

```
conda activate diffusionsdf
```

```

conda activate diffusionsdf
```

Content:
### Download Pre-trained Models
Download the SDF auto-encoder model file (vae_epoch-120.pth: [Baidu Disk](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1z0eh8SXSrn8tbq5epo0r6Q?pwd=f1cv) / [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/18MxWYf6IItYOxUzdM5LiWb8dr9zSwA-2/view?usp=sharing)) and the Voxelized Diffusion model file (voxdiff-uinu.ckpt: [Baidu Disk](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1Emu5kFVaYbuKIkdCKlghXQ?pwd=q1wv) / [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1yeB0dJGZvIXdF1V1DhI-fRz6CKnGbIwJ/view?usp=sharing))) from the above links. Place the downloaded model files in the directory ```./ckpt``` .

---

## Inference

### Text-to-Shape Generation
To generate 3D shapes from text descriptions using Diffusion-SDF, run:
Translation:
### Descargar Modelos Preentrenados
Descargue el archivo del modelo auto-codificador SDF (vae_epoch-120.pth: [Baidu Disk](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1z0eh8SXSrn8tbq5epo0r6Q?pwd=f1cv) / [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/18MxWYf6IItYOxUzdM5LiWb8dr9zSwA-2/view?usp=sharing)) y el archivo del modelo de Difusión Voxelizada (voxdiff-uinu.ckpt: [Baidu Disk](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1Emu5kFVaYbuKIkdCKlghXQ?pwd=q1wv) / [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1yeB0dJGZvIXdF1V1DhI-fRz6CKnGbIwJ/view?usp=sharing))) desde los enlaces anteriores. Coloque los archivos de modelo descargados en el directorio ```./ckpt``` .

---

## Inferencia

### Generación de Forma desde Texto
Para generar formas 3D a partir de descripciones de texto usando Diffusion-SDF, ejecute:

Place the downloaded model files in the directory ```./ckpt``` .

Coloque los archivos de modelo descargados en el directorio ```./ckpt``` .

RowTranslations.translatedContent: Para generar formas 3D a partir de descripciones de texto usando Diffusion-SDF, ejecute:

Content:
```
python txt2sdf.py --prompt "a revolving chair" --save_obj
```
Translation:
```
python txt2sdf.py --prompt "a revolving chair" --save_obj
```

Content:
The generated 3D shape will be saved as GIF renderings and OBJ files under `outputs/`.

### Text-Conditioned Shape Completion
Given a partial/incomplete 3D shape (as an `.h5` SDF file) and a text prompt, Diffusion-SDF can complete the missing regions:
Translation:
La forma 3D generada se guardará como renderizados GIF y archivos OBJ en `outputs/`.

### Completado de Forma Condicionado por Texto
Dada una forma 3D parcial/incompleta (como un archivo `.h5` SDF) y un texto de indicación, Diffusion-SDF puede completar las regiones faltantes:

RowTranslations.originContent: The generated 3D shape will be saved as GIF renderings and OBJ files under `outputs/`.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: La forma 3D generada se guardará como renderizados GIF y archivos OBJ en `outputs/`.
### Completado de Forma Condicionado por Texto

RowTranslations.originContent: Given a partial/incomplete 3D shape (as an `.h5` SDF file) and a text prompt, Diffusion-SDF can complete the missing regions:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Dada una forma 3D parcial/incompleta (como un archivo `.h5` SDF) y un texto de indicación, Diffusion-SDF puede completar las regiones faltantes:

Content:
```bash
# Axial cut: mask out the bottom half along the Z axis
python shape_completion.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/partial.h5 \
    --prompt "a wooden chair" \
    --mask_axis z --mask_ratio 0.5

# SDF-value based masking (mask voxels with SDF >= threshold)
python shape_completion.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/shape.h5 \
    --prompt "a dining table" \
    --mask_type threshold --mask_value 0.0
```
Translation:
```bash
# Axial cut: mask out the bottom half along the Z axis
python shape_completion.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/partial.h5 \
    --prompt "a wooden chair" \
    --mask_axis z --mask_ratio 0.5

# SDF-value based masking (mask voxels with SDF >= threshold)
python shape_completion.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/shape.h5 \
    --prompt "a dining table" \
    --mask_type threshold --mask_value 0.0
```

RowTranslations.originContent: # Axial cut: mask out the bottom half along the Z axis
RowTranslations.translatedContent: # Axial cut: mask out the bottom half along the Z axis

RowTranslations.originContent: # SDF-value based masking (mask voxels with SDF >= threshold)
RowTranslations.translatedContent: # SDF-value based masking (mask voxels with SDF >= threshold)

Content:
Results (GIF renderings and optional OBJ files) are saved under `outputs/shape_completion/`.

### Text-Conditioned Shape Manipulation
Given an existing 3D shape and a text prompt, Diffusion-SDF modifies the shape via the SDEdit approach — encoding the shape to latent space, adding noise up to a chosen timestep, then denoising with the new text prompt:
Translation:
Los resultados (representaciones GIF y archivos OBJ opcionales) se guardan en `outputs/shape_completion/`.

### Manipulación de formas condicionada por texto
Dada una forma 3D existente y un texto de entrada, Diffusion-SDF modifica la forma mediante el enfoque SDEdit: codificando la forma en el espacio latente, añadiendo ruido hasta un paso temporal elegido, y luego eliminando el ruido con el nuevo texto de entrada:

RowTranslations.originContent: Results (GIF renderings and optional OBJ files) are saved under `outputs/shape_completion/`.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Los resultados (representaciones GIF y archivos OBJ opcionales) se guardan en `outputs/shape_completion/`.
### Manipulación de formas condicionada por texto

RowTranslations.originContent: Given an existing 3D shape and a text prompt, Diffusion-SDF modifies the shape via the SDEdit approach — encoding the shape to latent space, adding noise up to a chosen timestep, then denoising with the new text prompt:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Dada una forma 3D existente y un texto de entrada, Diffusion-SDF modifica la forma mediante el enfoque SDEdit: codificando la forma en el espacio latente, añadiendo ruido hasta un paso temporal elegido, y luego eliminando el ruido con el nuevo texto de entrada:

Content:
```bash
# Moderate manipulation (50% noise strength)
python shape_manipulation.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/shape.h5 \
    --prompt "a chair with a cushion" \
    --strength 0.5

# Strong manipulation (75% noise strength — more creative freedom)
python shape_manipulation.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/shape.h5 \
    --prompt "a modern minimalist chair" \
    --strength 0.75
```
Translation:
```bash
# Moderate manipulation (50% noise strength)
python shape_manipulation.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/shape.h5 \
    --prompt "a chair with a cushion" \
    --strength 0.5

# Strong manipulation (75% noise strength — more creative freedom)
python shape_manipulation.py \
    --input_sdf path/to/shape.h5 \
    --prompt "a modern minimalist chair" \
    --strength 0.75
```

RowTranslations.originContent: # Strong manipulation (75% noise strength — more creative freedom)
RowTranslations.translatedContent: # Strong manipulation (75% noise strength — more creative freedom)

Content:
Results are saved under `outputs/shape_manipulation/`, including a rendering of the original shape for comparison.

---

## Training

### Data Preparation

Training requires two things: voxelized SDF files for the 3D shapes, and text captions from Text2Shape.

#### Step 0 — Download ShapeNet Core v1

Register and download [ShapeNet Core v1](https://shapenet.org/) and extract it somewhere (e.g. `data/ShapeNetCore.v1/`).

#### Step 1 — Precompute 64³ SDF volumes

ShapeNet provides triangle meshes; the autoencoder and diffusion model need voxelized signed-distance fields on a 64³ grid, stored as HDF5 files. We follow the same preprocessing pipeline as [SDFusion](https://github.com/yccyenchicheng/SDFusion):
Translation:
Los resultados se guardan en `outputs/shape_manipulation/`, incluyendo una renderización de la forma original para comparación.

---

## Entrenamiento

### Preparación de Datos

El entrenamiento requiere dos cosas: archivos SDF voxelizados para las formas 3D, y descripciones textuales de Text2Shape.

#### Paso 0 — Descargar ShapeNet Core v1

Regístrese y descargue [ShapeNet Core v1](https://shapenet.org/) y extráigalo en algún lugar (por ejemplo, `data/ShapeNetCore.v1/`).

#### Paso 1 — Precalcular volúmenes SDF de 64³

ShapeNet proporciona mallas triangulares; el autoencoder y el modelo de difusión necesitan campos de distancia firmada voxelizados en una cuadrícula de 64³, almacenados como archivos HDF5. Seguimos la misma cadena de preprocesamiento que [SDFusion](https://github.com/yccyenchicheng/SDFusion):

RowTranslations.originContent: Results are saved under `outputs/shape_manipulation/`, including a rendering of the original shape for comparison.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Los resultados se guardan en `outputs/shape_manipulation/`, incluyendo una renderización de la forma original para comparación.

RowTranslations.originContent: Training requires two things: voxelized SDF files for the 3D shapes, and text captions from Text2Shape.
RowTranslations.translatedContent: El entrenamiento requiere dos cosas: archivos SDF voxelizados para las formas 3D, y descripciones textuales de Text2Shape.

RowTranslations.originContent: Register and download [ShapeNet Core v1](https://shapenet.org/) and extract it somewhere (e.g. `data/ShapeNetCore.v1/`).
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Regístrese y descargue [ShapeNet Core v1](https://shapenet.org/) y extráigalo en algún lugar (por ejemplo, `data/ShapeNetCore.v1/`).

RowTranslations.originContent: ShapeNet provides triangle meshes; the autoencoder and diffusion model need voxelized signed-distance fields on a 64³ grid, stored as HDF5 files. We follow the same preprocessing pipeline as [SDFusion](https://github.com/yccyenchicheng/SDFusion):
RowTranslations.translatedContent: ShapeNet proporciona mallas triangulares; el autoencoder y el modelo de difusión necesitan campos de distancia firmada voxelizados en una cuadrícula de 64³, almacenados como archivos HDF5. Seguimos la misma cadena de preprocesamiento que [SDFusion](https://github.com/yccyenchicheng/SDFusion):

Content:
```bash
# Install system dependencies (Ubuntu/Debian)
sudo apt-get install freeglut3-dev libtbb-dev

# Clone SDFusion and run their SDF generation scripts
# (see SDFusion repo for the full launcher scripts)
cd preprocess
bash launch_create_sdf_shapenet.sh \
    --shapenet_root data/ShapeNetCore.v1 \
    --out_root data/ShapeNet/sdf
```
Translation:
```bash
# Install system dependencies (Ubuntu/Debian)
sudo apt-get install freeglut3-dev libtbb-dev

# Clone SDFusion and run their SDF generation scripts
# (see SDFusion repo for the full launcher scripts)
cd preprocess
bash launch_create_sdf_shapenet.sh \
    --shapenet_root data/ShapeNetCore.v1 \
    --out_root data/ShapeNet/sdf
```

RowTranslations.originContent: # Clone SDFusion and run their SDF generation scripts
RowTranslations.translatedContent: # Clone SDFusion and run their SDF generation scripts

RowTranslations.originContent: # (see SDFusion repo for the full launcher scripts)
RowTranslations.translatedContent: # (see SDFusion repo for the full launcher scripts)

Content: The expected output layout is:
Translation: El diseño de salida esperado es:

RowTranslations.translatedContent: El diseño de salida esperado es:

Content:
```
data/ShapeNet/
  sdf/
    <synset_id>/          e.g. 03001627 (chair), 04379243 (table)
      <model_id>/
        pc_sdf_sample.h5  float32 array of shape (262144,) = 64³ SDF values
```
Translation:
```
data/ShapeNet/
  sdf/
    <synset_id>/          e.g. 03001627 (chair), 04379243 (table)
      <model_id>/
        pc_sdf_sample.h5  float32 array of shape (262144,) = 64³ SDF values
```

RowTranslations.originContent: pc_sdf_sample.h5  float32 array of shape (262144,) = 64³ SDF values
RowTranslations.translatedContent: pc_sdf_sample.h5  float32 array of shape (262144,) = 64³ SDF values

Content:
The HDF5 key is `pc_sdf_sample` and the array is stored flat (262144 = 64×64×64 elements).

#### Step 2 — Prepare Text2Shape captions

[Text2Shape](http://text2shape.stanford.edu/) provides natural-language descriptions for ShapeNet **chairs** and **tables** only. Other categories will be trained unconditionally (empty caption).
Translation:
La clave HDF5 es `pc_sdf_sample` y el arreglo se almacena plano (262144 = 64×64×64 elementos).

#### Paso 2 — Preparar los captions de Text2Shape

[Text2Shape](http://text2shape.stanford.edu/) proporciona descripciones en lenguaje natural solo para las **sillas** y **mesas** de ShapeNet. Otras categorías se entrenarán de forma incondicional (caption vacío).

RowTranslations.originContent: The HDF5 key is `pc_sdf_sample` and the array is stored flat (262144 = 64×64×64 elements).
RowTranslations.translatedContent: La clave HDF5 es `pc_sdf_sample` y el arreglo se almacena plano (262144 = 64×64×64 elementos).
#### Paso 2 — Preparar los captions de Text2Shape

RowTranslations.originContent: [Text2Shape](http://text2shape.stanford.edu/) provides natural-language descriptions for ShapeNet **chairs** and **tables** only. Other categories will be trained unconditionally (empty caption).
RowTranslations.translatedContent: [Text2Shape](http://text2shape.stanford.edu/) proporciona descripciones en lenguaje natural solo para las **sillas** y **mesas** de ShapeNet. Otras categorías se entrenarán de forma incondicional (caption vacío).

Content:
```bash
# Download the caption CSV
mkdir -p data/ShapeNet/text
wget http://text2shape.stanford.edu/dataset/captions.tablechair.csv \
    -O data/ShapeNet/text/captions.tablechair.csv

# Convert to captions.json and generate train/val/test splits
python preprocess/prepare_text2shape.py --data_root data/ShapeNet
```
Translation:
```bash
# Download the caption CSV
mkdir -p data/ShapeNet/text
wget http://text2shape.stanford.edu/dataset/captions.tablechair.csv \
    -O data/ShapeNet/text/captions.tablechair.csv

# Convert to captions.json and generate train/val/test splits
python preprocess/prepare_text2shape.py --data_root data/ShapeNet
```

RowTranslations.originContent: # Convert to captions.json and generate train/val/test splits
RowTranslations.translatedContent: # Convert to captions.json and generate train/val/test splits

Content: This produces:

```
data/ShapeNet/
  text/
    captions.tablechair.csv   (raw Text2Shape CSV)
    captions.json             {model_id: [caption, ...]}
  train_models.json           [model_id, ...]
  val_models.json
  test_models.json
```


Content: If you have ShapeNet's official split JSON files, pass them with `--shapenet_split_dir` to use the canonical splits instead of a random split:
Translation: Si tienes los archivos JSON de división oficial de ShapeNet, pásalos con `--shapenet_split_dir` para usar las divisiones canónicas en lugar de una división aleatoria:

RowTranslations.originContent: If you have ShapeNet's official split JSON files, pass them with `--shapenet_split_dir` to use the canonical splits instead of a random split:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Si tienes los archivos JSON de división oficial de ShapeNet, pásalos con `--shapenet_split_dir` para usar las divisiones canónicas en lugar de una división aleatoria:

Content:
```bash
python preprocess/prepare_text2shape.py \
    --data_root data/ShapeNet \
    --shapenet_split_dir data/ShapeNet/splits
```
Translation:
```bash
python preprocess/prepare_text2shape.py \
    --data_root data/ShapeNet \
    --shapenet_split_dir data/ShapeNet/splits
```

Content:
### Step 1 — Train the SDF Autoencoder
Train the patch-wise variational autoencoder that encodes 64³ SDF volumes into a compact 8³ latent space:
Translation:
### Paso 1 — Entrenar el Autoencoder SDF
Entrena el autoencoder variacional por parches que codifica volúmenes SDF de 64³ en un espacio latente compacto de 8³:

RowTranslations.originContent: Train the patch-wise variational autoencoder that encodes 64³ SDF volumes into a compact 8³ latent space:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Entrena el autoencoder variacional por parches que codifica volúmenes SDF de 64³ en un espacio latente compacto de 8³:

Content:
```bash
# Single GPU
python train_ae.py --data_root data/ShapeNet --cat all

# Resume from a checkpoint
python train_ae.py --data_root data/ShapeNet \
    --resume ckpt/vae_epoch-120.pth --start_epoch 121

# Multi-GPU (DDP via torchrun)
torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_ae.py --data_root data/ShapeNet --dist_train
```
Translation:
```bash
# Single GPU
python train_ae.py --data_root data/ShapeNet --cat all

# Resume from a checkpoint
python train_ae.py --data_root data/ShapeNet \
    --resume ckpt/vae_epoch-120.pth --start_epoch 121

# Multi-GPU (DDP via torchrun)
torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_ae.py --data_root data/ShapeNet --dist_train
```

Content:
Checkpoints are saved to `./ckpt/` as `vae_epoch-{N}.pth`.

### Step 2 — Train the Voxelized Diffusion Model
After the AE is trained, train the text-conditioned 3D diffusion model using PyTorch Lightning:
Translation:
Los puntos de control se guardan en `./ckpt/` como `vae_epoch-{N}.pth`.

### Paso 2 — Entrenar el Modelo de Difusión Voxelizado
Después de entrenar el AE, entrena el modelo de difusión 3D condicionado por texto usando PyTorch Lightning:

RowTranslations.translatedContent: Los puntos de control se guardan en `./ckpt/` como `vae_epoch-{N}.pth`.

RowTranslations.originContent: ### Step 2 — Train the Voxelized Diffusion Model
RowTranslations.translatedContent: ### Paso 2 — Entrenar el Modelo de Difusión Voxelizado

RowTranslations.originContent: After the AE is trained, train the text-conditioned 3D diffusion model using PyTorch Lightning:
RowTranslations.translatedContent: Después de entrenar el AE, entrena el modelo de difusión 3D condicionado por texto usando PyTorch Lightning:

Content:
```bash
# Single GPU
python main.py --config configs/voxdiff-uinu.yaml

# Resume from a checkpoint
python main.py --config configs/voxdiff-uinu.yaml --resume /path/to/checkpoint.ckpt

# Multi-GPU
python main.py --config configs/voxdiff-uinu.yaml --gpus 0,1,2,3
```
Translation:
```bash
# Single GPU

# Resume from a checkpoint
python main.py --config configs/voxdiff-uinu.yaml --resume /path/to/checkpoint.ckpt

# Multi-GPU
python main.py --config configs/voxdiff-uinu.yaml --gpus 0,1,2,3
```

Content:
Checkpoints are saved under `logs/<run_name>/checkpoints/`.
